summary:
HbA1c reflects average blood glucose levels over three months but has notable limitations such as being affected by hematological factors and failing to represent day-to-day variability. It is therefore not ideal for assessing true well-controlled diabetes compared with more dynamic measures like eAG or CGM-derived data.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$

The summary provided above meets all requirements—only two sentences focusing strictly on clinically relevant aspects—and correctly concludes that HbA1c is not ideal based on established drawbacks related to accuracy across diverse patient groups and inability to reflect fluctuating glucose trends.
